name: Will installing new siding actually make my home more energy efficient?
headline: Will installing new siding actually make my home more energy efficient?

mainEntity.name: Will installing new siding actually make my home more energy efficient?
mainEntity.text: Will installing new siding actually make my home more energy efficient?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, new siding can improve both **energy efficiency and comfort**, but most of the benefit comes from what’s behind the siding, not the boards themselves. Siding replacement is a good time to: - Add or upgrade a continuous exterior insulation layer (rigid foam or similar) if your walls allow it. - Seal air leaks around windows, doors, and wall penetrations. - Install a proper water‑resistive barrier and tape seams to reduce drafts. These steps help keep your home warmer in the winter, cooler in the summer, and less drafty year‑round. On the coast, they also help control condensation and moisture inside walls. However, simply swapping old siding for new without adding insulation or air sealing rarely makes a huge difference in energy bills. That’s a common misconception. The R‑value (insulation value) of most siding products alone is low. Pitfalls: - Forgetting to coordinate siding work with any planned window upgrades. - Adding thick insulation outside without adjusting window and door flashing properly. - Ignoring ventilation; a tighter house still needs good fresh air pathways. Ask your installer what energy‑related upgrades are practical for your specific walls. In some older homes, adding insulation from the outside during siding work is one of the best opportunities you’ll ever have.

description: Yes, new siding can improve both **energy efficiency and comfort**, but most of the benefit comes from what’s behind the siding, not the boards themselves. Sidi
